sentences.text: The ext:zcmp[] extension adds instructions that substantially reduce the static code size of procedure prologues and epilogues.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues

sentences.text: The instructions it adds are collectively referred to as PUSH/POP:
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues

sentences.text: The term PUSH refers to cm.push.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues

sentences.text: The term POP refers to cm.pop.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues

sentences.text: The term POPRET refers to cm.popret and cm.popretz.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues

sentences.text: Common details for these instructions are in this section.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues

sentences.text: PUSH, POP, POPRET are used to reduce the size of function prologues and epilogues.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview

sentences.text: The PUSH instruction ** adjusts the stack pointer to create the stack frame ** pushes (stores) the registers specified in the register list to the stack frame
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview

sentences.text: The POP instruction ** pops (loads) the registers in the register list from the stack frame ** adjusts the stack pointer to destroy the stack frame
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview

sentences.text: The POPRET instructions ** pop (load) the registers in the register list from the stack frame ** cm.popretz also moves zero into a0 as the return value ** adjust the stack pointer to destroy the stack frame ** execute a ret instruction to return from the function
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview

sentences.text: This example gives an illustration of the use of PUSH and POPRET.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: The function processMarkers in the EMBench benchmark picojpeg in the following file on github: https://github.com/embench/embench-iot/blob/master/src/picojpeg/libpicojpeg.c[libpicojpeg.c]
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: The prologue and epilogue compile with GCC10 to:
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: 0001098a <processMarkers>: 1098a: 711d addi sp,sp,-96 ;cm.push(1) 1098c: c8ca sw s2,80(sp) ;cm.push(2) 1098e: c6ce sw s3,76(sp) ;cm.push(3) 10990: c4d2 sw s4,72(sp) ;cm.push(4) 10992: ce86 sw ra,92(sp) ;cm.push(5) 10994: cca2 sw s0,88(sp) ;cm.push(6) 10996: caa6 sw s1,84(sp) ;cm.push(7) 10998: c2d6 sw s5,68(sp) ;cm.push(8) 1099a: c0da sw s6,64(sp) ;cm.push(9) 1099c: de5e sw s7,60(sp) ;cm.push(10) 1099e: dc62 sw s8,56(sp) ;cm.push(11) 109a0: da66 sw s9,52(sp) ;cm.push(12) 109a2: d86a sw s10,48(sp);cm.push(13) 109a4: d66e sw s11,44(sp);cm.push(14) ... 109f4: 4501 li a0,0 ;cm.popretz(1) 109f6: 40f6 lw ra,92(sp) ;cm.popretz(2) 109f8: 4466 lw s0,88(sp) ;cm.popretz(3) 109fa: 44d6 lw s1,84(sp) ;cm.popretz(4) 109fc: 4946 lw s2,80(sp) ;cm.popretz(5) 109fe: 49b6 lw s3,76(sp) ;cm.popretz(6) 10a00: 4a26 lw s4,72(sp) ;cm.popretz(7) 10a02: 4a96 lw s5,68(sp) ;cm.popretz(8) 10a04: 4b06 lw s6,64(sp) ;cm.popretz(9) 10a06: 5bf2 lw s7,60(sp) ;cm.popretz(10) 10a08: 5c62 lw s8,56(sp) ;cm.popretz(11) 10a0a: 5cd2 lw s9,52(sp) ;cm.popretz(12) 10a0c: 5d42 lw s10,48(sp);cm.popretz(13) 10a0e: 5db2 lw s11,44(sp);cm.popretz(14) 10a10: 6125 addi sp,sp,96 ;cm.popretz(15) 10a12: 8082 ret ;cm.popretz(16) ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: with the GCC option -msave-restore the output is the following:
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- 0001080e <processMarkers>: 1080e: 73a012ef jal t0,11f48 <riscvsave12> 10812: 1101 addi sp,sp,-32 ... 10862: 4501 li a0,0 10864: 6105 addi sp,sp,32 10866: 71e0106f j 11f84 <riscvrestore12> ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: with PUSH/POPRET this reduces to
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- 0001080e <processMarkers>: 1080e: b8fa cm.push \{ra,s0-s11},-96 ... 10866: bcfa cm.popretz \{ra,s0-s11}, 96 ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: The prologue / epilogue reduce from 60-bytes in the original code, to 14-bytes with -msave-restore, and to 4-bytes with PUSH and POPRET.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: As well as reducing the code-size PUSH and POPRET eliminate the branches from calling the millicode save/restore routines and so may also perform better.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage

sentences.text: The instructions all automatically adjust the stack pointer by enough to cover the memory required for the registers being saved or restored.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage > Stack pointer adjustment handling

sentences.text: Additionally the spimm field in the encoding allows the stack pointer to be adjusted in additional increments of 16-bytes.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage > Stack pointer adjustment handling

sentences.text: There is only a small restricted range available in the encoding; if the range is insufficient then a separate c.addi16sp can be used to increase the range.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage > Stack pointer adjustment handling

sentences.text: There is no support for the \{ra, s0-s10} register list without also adding s11.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage > Register list handling

sentences.text: Therefore the \{ra, s0-s11} register list must be used in this case.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage > Register list handling

sentences.text: [pushpop-idempotent-memory]
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example usage > Register list handling

sentences.text: Correct execution requires that sp refers to idempotent memory (also see ), because the core must be able to handle traps detected during the sequence.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > PUSH/POP Fault handling

sentences.text: The entire PUSH/POP sequence is re-executed after returning from the trap handler, and multiple traps are possible during the sequence.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > PUSH/POP Fault handling

sentences.text: If a trap occurs during the sequence then xEPC is updated with the PC of the instruction, xTVAL (if not read-only-zero) updated with the bad address if it was an access fault and xCAUSE updated with the type of trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > PUSH/POP Fault handling

sentences.text: NOTE: It is implementation defined whether interrupts can also be taken during the sequence execution.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > PUSH/POP Fault handling

sentences.text: From a software perspective the PUSH sequence appears as:
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: A sequence of stores writing the bytes required by the pseudocode ** The bytes may be written in any order. ** The bytes may be grouped into larger accesses. ** Any of the bytes may be written multiple times. * A stack pointer adjustment
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: The stack pointer adjustment must only be committed only when it is certain that the entire PUSH instruction will commit.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: Stores may also return imprecise faults from the bus.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: It is platform defined whether the core implementation waits for the bus responses before continuing to the final stage of the sequence, or handles errors responses after completing the PUSH instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- cm.push \{ra, s0-s5}, -64 ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- any bytes from sp-1 to sp-28 may be written multiple times before the instruction completes therefore these updates may be visible in the interrupt/exception handler below the stack pointer sw s5, -4(sp) sw s4, -8(sp) sw s3,-12(sp) sw s2,-16(sp) sw s1,-20(sp) sw s0,-24(sp) sw ra,-28(sp)
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: this must only execute once, and will only execute after all stores completed without any precise faults, therefore this update is only visible in the interrupt/exception handler if cm.push has completed addi sp, sp, -64 ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: From a software perspective the POP/POPRET sequence appears as:
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: A sequence of loads reading the bytes required by the pseudocode. ** The bytes may be loaded in any order. ** The bytes may be grouped into larger accesses. ** Any of the bytes may be loaded multiple times. * A stack pointer adjustment * An optional `li a0, 0` * An optional `ret`
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: If a trap occurs during the sequence, then any loads which were executed before the trap may update architectural state.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: The loads will be re-executed once the trap handler completes, so the values will be overwritten.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: Therefore it is permitted for an implementation to update some of the destination registers before taking a fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: The optional `li a0, 0`, stack pointer adjustment and optional `ret` must only be committed only when it is certain that the entire POP/POPRET instruction will commit.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: For POPRET once the stack pointer adjustment has been committed the `ret` must execute.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- cm.popretz \{ra, s0-s3}, 32; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- any or all of these load instructions may execute multiple times therefore these updates may be visible in the interrupt/exception handler lw s3, 28(sp) lw s2, 24(sp) lw s1, 20(sp) lw s0, 16(sp) lw ra, 12(sp)
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: these must only execute once, will only execute after all loads complete successfully all instructions must execute atomically therefore these updates are not visible in the interrupt/exception handler li a0, 0 addi sp, sp, 32 ret ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: An implementation may have a requirement to issue a PUSH/POP instruction to non-idempotent memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Non-idempotent memory handling

sentences.text: If the core implementation does not support PUSH/POP to non-idempotent memories, the core may use an idempotency PMA to detect it and take a load (POP/POPRET) or store (PUSH) access-fault exception in order to avoid unpredictable results.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Non-idempotent memory handling

sentences.text: Software should only use these instructions on non-idempotent memory regions when software can tolerate the required memory accesses being issued repeatedly in the case that they cause exceptions.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Non-idempotent memory handling

sentences.text: The examples are included show the load/store series expansion and the stack adjustment.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences

sentences.text: Examples of cm.popret and cm.popretz are not included, as the difference in the expanded sequence from cm.pop is trivial in all cases.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences

sentences.text: Encoding: rlist=7, spimm=3
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.push  \{ra, s0-s2}, -64

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- sw s2, -4(sp); sw s1, -8(sp); sw s0, -12(sp); sw ra, -16(sp); addi sp, sp, -64; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.push  \{ra, s0-s2}, -64

sentences.text: Encoding: rlist=15, spimm=3
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.push \{ra, s0-s11}, -112

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- sw s11, -4(sp); sw s10, -8(sp); sw s9, -12(sp); sw s8, -16(sp); sw s7, -20(sp); sw s6, -24(sp); sw s5, -28(sp); sw s4, -32(sp); sw s3, -36(sp); sw s2, -40(sp); sw s1, -44(sp); sw s0, -48(sp); sw ra, -52(sp); addi sp, sp, -112; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.push \{ra, s0-s11}, -112

sentences.text: Encoding: rlist=4, spimm=0
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.pop   \{ra}, 16

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- lw ra, 12(sp); addi sp, sp, 16; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.pop   \{ra}, 16

sentences.text: Encoding: rlist=8, spimm=1
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.pop \{ra, s0-s3}, 48

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- lw s3, 44(sp); lw s2, 40(sp); lw s1, 36(sp); lw s0, 32(sp); lw ra, 28(sp); addi sp, sp, 48; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.pop \{ra, s0-s3}, 48

sentences.text: Encoding: rlist=9, spimm=2
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.pop \{ra, s0-s4}, 64

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- lw s4, 60(sp); lw s3, 56(sp); lw s2, 52(sp); lw s1, 48(sp); lw s0, 44(sp); lw ra, 40(sp); addi sp, sp, 64; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.pop \{ra, s0-s4}, 64

sentences.text: <<< [insns-cmpush,reftext="cm.push"]
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > Example RV32I PUSH/POP sequences > cm.pop \{ra, s0-s4}, 64

sentences.text: Create stack frame: store ra and 0 to 12 saved registers to the stack frame, optionally allocate additional stack space.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: cm.push \{reglist}, -stackadj
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg:[ { bits: 2, name: 0x2, attr: ['C2'] }, { bits: 2, name: 'spimm', attr: [] }, { bits: 4, name: 'rlist', attr: [] }, { bits: 5, name: 0x18, attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 0x5, attr: ['FUNCT3'] }, ],config:{bits:16}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: [source,asm] -- cm.push \{reglist}, -stackadj cm.push {xreglist}, -stackadj --
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: The variables used in the assembly syntax are defined below.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: switch (rlist){ case 4: \{reglist="ra"; xreglist="x1";} case 5: \{reglist="ra, s0"; xreglist="x1, x8";} case 6: \{reglist="ra, s0-s1"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9";} default: reserved(); } stackadj = stackadjbase + spimm * 16; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: [source,sail] ---- RV32I, RV64: switch (rlist){ case 4: \{reglist="ra"; xreglist="x1";} case 5: \{reglist="ra, s0"; xreglist="x1, x8";} case 6: \{reglist="ra, s0-s1"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9";} case 7: \{reglist="ra, s0-s2"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18";} case 8: \{reglist="ra, s0-s3"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x19";} case 9: \{reglist="ra, s0-s4"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x20";} case 10: \{reglist="ra, s0-s5"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x21";} case 11: \{reglist="ra, s0-s6"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x22";} case 12: \{reglist="ra, s0-s7"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x23";} case 13: \{reglist="ra, s0-s8"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x24";} case 14: \{reglist="ra, s0-s9"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x25";} //note - to include s10, s11 must also be included case 15: \{reglist="ra, s0-s11"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x27";} default: reserved(); } stackadj = stackadjbase + spimm * 16; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: Valid values: stackadj = [16|32|48|64]; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: switch (rlist) { case 4.. 7: stackadjbase = 16; case 8..11: stackadjbase = 32; case 12..14: stackadjbase = 48; case 15: stackadjbase = 64; }
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: Valid values: switch (rlist) { case 4.. 7: stackadj = [16|32|48| 64]; case 8..11: stackadj = [32|48|64| 80]; case 12..14: stackadj = [48|64|80| 96]; case 15: stackadj = [64|80|96|112]; } ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: switch (rlist) { case 4.. 5: stackadjbase = 16; case 6.. 7: stackadjbase = 32; case 8.. 9: stackadjbase = 48; case 10..11: stackadjbase = 64; case 12..13: stackadjbase = 80; case 14: stackadjbase = 96; case 15: stackadjbase = 112; }
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: Valid values: switch (rlist) { case 4.. 5: stackadj = [ 16| 32| 48| 64]; case 6.. 7: stackadj = [ 32| 48| 64| 80]; case 8.. 9: stackadj = [ 48| 64| 80| 96]; case 10..11: stackadj = [ 64| 80| 96|112]; case 12..13: stackadj = [ 80| 96|112|128]; case 14: stackadj = [ 96|112|128|144]; case 15: stackadj = [112|128|144|160]; } ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: This instruction pushes (stores) the registers in reglist to the memory below the stack pointer, and then creates the stack frame by decrementing the stack pointer by stackadj, including any additional stack space requested by the value of spimm.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: For further information see ext:zcmp[].
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: Stack Adjustment Calculation:
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: stackadjbase is the minimum number of bytes, in multiples of 16-byte address increments, required to cover the registers in the list.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: spimm is the number of additional 16-byte address increments allocated for the stack frame.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: The total stack adjustment represents the total size of the stack frame, which is stackadjbase added to spimm scaled by 16, as defined above.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.push

sentences.text: No direct equivalent encoding exists

sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- cm.pop \{reglist}, stackadj cm.pop {xreglist}, stackadj ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: The variables used in the assembly syntax are defined below.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: [source,sail] ---- RV32E: switch (rlist){ case 4: \{reglist="ra"; xreglist="x1";} case 5: \{reglist="ra, s0"; xreglist="x1, x8";} case 6: \{reglist="ra, s0-s1"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9";} default: reserved(); } stackadj = stackadjbase + spimm * 16; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: [source,sail] ---- RV32I, RV64: switch (rlist){ case 4: \{reglist="ra"; xreglist="x1";} case 5: \{reglist="ra, s0"; xreglist="x1, x8";} case 6: \{reglist="ra, s0-s1"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9";} case 7: \{reglist="ra, s0-s2"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18";} case 8: \{reglist="ra, s0-s3"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x19";} case 9: \{reglist="ra, s0-s4"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x20";} case 10: \{reglist="ra, s0-s5"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x21";} case 11: \{reglist="ra, s0-s6"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x22";} case 12: \{reglist="ra, s0-s7"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x23";} case 13: \{reglist="ra, s0-s8"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x24";} case 14: \{reglist="ra, s0-s9"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x25";} //note - to include s10, s11 must also be included case 15: \{reglist="ra, s0-s11"; xreglist="x1, x8-x9, x18-x27";} default: reserved(); } stackadj = stackadjbase + spimm * 16; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: Valid values: stackadj = [16|32|48|64]; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: switch (rlist) { case 4.. 7: stackadjbase = 16; case 8..11: stackadjbase = 32; case 12..14: stackadjbase = 48; case 15: stackadjbase = 64; }
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: Valid values: switch (rlist) { case 4.. 7: stackadj = [16|32|48| 64]; case 8..11: stackadj = [32|48|64| 80]; case 12..14: stackadj = [48|64|80| 96]; case 15: stackadj = [64|80|96|112]; } ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: switch (rlist) { case 4.. 5: stackadjbase = 16; case 6.. 7: stackadjbase = 32; case 8.. 9: stackadjbase = 48; case 10..11: stackadjbase = 64; case 12..13: stackadjbase = 80; case 14: stackadjbase = 96; case 15: stackadjbase = 112; }
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: Valid values: switch (rlist) { case 4.. 5: stackadj = [ 16| 32| 48| 64]; case 6.. 7: stackadj = [ 32| 48| 64| 80]; case 8.. 9: stackadj = [ 48| 64| 80| 96]; case 10..11: stackadj = [ 64| 80| 96|112]; case 12..13: stackadj = [ 80| 96|112|128]; case 14: stackadj = [ 96|112|128|144]; case 15: stackadj = [112|128|144|160]; } ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: This instruction pops (loads) the registers in reglist from stack memory, and then adjusts the stack pointer by stackadj.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: For further information see ext:zcmp[].
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: Stack Adjustment Calculation:
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: stackadjbase is the minimum number of bytes, in multiples of 16-byte address increments, required to cover the registers in the list.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: spimm is the number of additional 16-byte address increments allocated for the stack frame.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: The total stack adjustment represents the total size of the stack frame, which is stackadjbase added to spimm scaled by 16, as defined above.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.pop

sentences.text: For further information see ext:zcmp[].

sentences.text: Stack Adjustment Calculation:
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: stackadjbase is the minimum number of bytes, in multiples of 16-byte address increments, required to cover the registers in the list.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: spimm is the number of additional 16-byte address increments allocated for the stack frame.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: The total stack adjustment represents the total size of the stack frame, which is stackadjbase added to spimm scaled by 16, as defined above.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: No direct equivalent encoding exists
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: The first section of pseudocode may be executed multiple times before the instruction successfully completes.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: [source,sail] ---- //This is not SAIL, it's pseudocode.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: The SAIL hasn't been written yet.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: if (XLEN32) bytes=4; else bytes=8;
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: addr=sp+stackadj-bytes; for(i in 27,26,25,24,23,22,21,20,19,18,9,8,1) { //if register i is in xreglist if (xreglist[i]) { switch(bytes) { 4: asm("lw x[i], 0(addr)"); 8: asm("ld x[i], 0(addr)"); } addr-=bytes; } } ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: The final section of pseudocode executes atomically, and only executes if the section above completes without any exceptions or interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: [source,sail] ---- //This is not SAIL, it's pseudocode.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: The SAIL hasn't been written yet.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: sp+=stackadj; asm("ret"); ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: [insns-cmmvsa01,reftext="Move a0-a1 into two different s0-s7 registers"]
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.popret

sentences.text: Move a0-a1 into two registers of s0-s7
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg:[ { bits: 2, name: 0x2, attr: ['C2'] }, { bits: 3, name: 'r2s\'', attr: [] }, { bits: 2, name: 0x1, attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 'r1s\'', attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 0x3, attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 0x5, attr: ['FUNCT3'] }, ],config:{bits:16}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- cm.mvsa01 r1s', r2s' ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: Description:: This instruction moves a0 into r1s' and a1 into r2s'. r1s' and r2s' must be different.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: The execution is atomic, so it is not possible to observe state where only one of r1s' or r2s' has been updated.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: The encoding uses sreg number specifiers instead of xreg number specifiers to save encoding space.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: The mapping between them is specified in the pseudocode below.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: No direct equivalent encoding exists.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: [source,sail] ---- //This is not SAIL, it's pseudocode.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: The SAIL hasn't been written yet. if (RV32E && (r1sc>1 || r2sc>1)) { reserved(); } xreg1 = {r1sc[2:1]>0,r1sc[2:1]0,r1sc[2:0]}; xreg2 = {r2sc[2:1]>0,r2sc[2:1]0,r2sc[2:0]}; X[xreg1] = X[10]; X[xreg2] = X[11]; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: [insns-cmmva01s,reftext="Move two s0-s7 registers into a0-a1"]
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mvsa01

sentences.text: Move two s0-s7 registers into a0-a1
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg:[ { bits: 2, name: 0x2, attr: ['C2'] }, { bits: 3, name: 'r2s\'', attr: [] }, { bits: 2, name: 0x3, attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 'r1s\'', attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 0x3, attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 0x5, attr: ['FUNCT3'] }, ],config:{bits:16}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- cm.mva01s r1s', r2s' ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: Description:: This instruction moves r1s' into a0 and r2s' into a1.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: The execution is atomic, so it is not possible to observe state where only one of a0 or a1 have been updated.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: The encoding uses sreg number specifiers instead of xreg number specifiers to save encoding space.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: The mapping between them is specified in the pseudocode below.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: No direct equivalent encoding exists.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: [source,sail] ---- //This is not SAIL, it's pseudocode.
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: The SAIL hasn't been written yet. if (RV32E && (r1sc>1 || r2sc>1)) { reserved(); } xreg1 = {r1sc[2:1]>0,r1sc[2:1]0,r1sc[2:0]}; xreg2 = {r2sc[2:1]>0,r2sc[2:1]0,r2sc[2:0]}; X[10] = X[xreg1]; X[11] = X[xreg2]; ----
sentences.section: Preamble > ext:zcmp[] Extension for Compressed Prologues and Epilogues > PUSH/POP functional overview > cm.mva01s
